hypothesis: If we combine fragment-based polyamine generation with a two-stage MD+GCMC evaluation parameterized by DFT-refined polymer–CO2–H2O interactions and guide candidate selection via Bayesian optimization using pretrained molecular embeddings, then we will discover top-quantile CO2-adsorbing polyamines with significantly fewer simulations than random search, because improved interaction parameters and informative representations yield more accurate surrogates and more sample-efficient exploration.
